type: B2B hospitality SaaS — QR/app at-table ordering & order management for restaurants/bars (web + iOS/Android), UK
one_line_reason: Right shape (a real UK web+mobile hospitality SaaS you could operate) but the #1 growth lever is B2B restaurant/bar sales — your weakness — in a saturated market owned by POS incumbents (Toast/Square/SpotOn) and specialists (Sunday, me&u); it's order-management-only (no payments), shows no disclosed traction, and is over budget. growth_lever_fits=false → PASS (provisional, revenue unverified).

operability_upside.note: Real web + native mobile SaaS on your stack — you can run/maintain it. But it's a mature commodity: little product you could add moves the needle; the bottleneck is sales, not features.

pmf_retention.note: No customers/revenue disclosed; 'domain + software for $4k' phrasing implies ~zero traction, and restaurant tools churn hard. Unverified — decisive unknown.

unit_economics.note: Model is fine ($49.99/mo or $399.99/yr per venue, recurring — verified on pricing page) but actual current revenue is unknown and almost certainly negligible.

moat_defensibility.note: Near-zero moat. Commodity QR ordering with NO payments (rides existing till), so it loses to POS-bundled QR from Toast/Square/SpotOn and to Sunday/me&u.

growth_market.note: Digital ordering is growing, but this segment is saturated and owned by POS incumbents; a standalone no-payments tool is on the wrong side of that.

tech_ip.note: Real transferable asset (web + iOS + Android apps + backend + domain, UK Ltd) — more than a content site. Native apps mean ongoing maintenance + App Store/Play account transfer to confirm.

transfer_key_person.note: Assets (code/domain/apps) are impersonal and transfer — but with ~no customer base there's little growth engine to inherit either way.

deal_terms.note: $4,000 for a no-revenue commodity codebase a team like yours could rebuild — poor value — and over the $3k budget (1.3x).

seller_adverse_selection.note: 'Domain + software for sale', no revenue mentioned = built-but-couldn't-sell in a hard market. Not fraud; just likely no traction.

key_unknowns: Any paying venues / MRR at all? The Reddit r/saasforsale post body was never read (reddit.com on the fetch blocklist); the 'domain + software for $4k' phrasing suggests ~zero, but this is the decisive unknown.
Why selling, and what's included beyond domain + code (iOS/Android app-store listings + developer accounts? the RoomDrop sibling product? the TableDrop LTD entity?).
Is there ANY non-sales acquisition channel (a POS partnership, a directory, inbound) — or is it 100% cold B2B hospitality sales?
Code/IP ownership + clean transfer of the App Store / Google Play developer accounts (native apps).
Ongoing costs (hosting, app-store fees, any third-party APIs).
seller_questions: How many paying venues are live today, MRR, and churn — screen-shared from Stripe/billing? (PMF + revenue; the decisive unknown)
Why are you selling, and how did you acquire the venues you have (cold sales, a POS partner, inbound)? (channel — does any non-sales lever exist?)
Exactly what's included: domain, full codebase, the iOS + Android apps AND their developer accounts, the RoomDrop sibling, the TableDrop LTD company? (IP/transfer)
Is 100% of the code and design owned by you (no license-limited templates), and will the app-store accounts transfer? (IP)
Monthly running costs and any third-party dependencies? (unit economics)
Any existing POS/payment integrations or partnerships that would help distribution? (moat/channel)
inputs_provided: Reddit r/saasforsale listing 'TableDrop.com – Domain and Software for sale ($4000)' [title provided by user; POST BODY NOT READ — reddit.com is on the fetch blocklist, so revenue/why-selling are UNKNOWN pending the user pasting them]

Product site: QR/app at-table ordering & order management for restaurants/bars; free customer app + QR; geolocation menu; order dashboard; works alongside existing payment/till systems (NOT payments); UK 'TableDrop LTD'; sibling product RoomDrop [VERIFIED via tabledrop.com]
Pricing: $49.99/mo or $399.99/yr per venue, 7-day trial, no contract [VERIFIED via tabledrop.com/pricing]

Market: QR at-table ordering is saturated/commoditized in 2026 — POS incumbents (Toast, Square, SpotOn, Eats365) bundle it; specialists (Sunday 3,500+ clients/80M+ tx/yr, me&u, TableQR, UpMenu) compete [VERIFIED via market research]
Asking $4,000 vs the team's $3,000 budget → affordability gate: over budget (suppressed in the verdict because merit is already PASS)
scorecard_markdown:
# Acquisition Scorecard — TableDrop.com (v2)

**Verdict:** PASS (provisional) — ~44 merit / completeness 49%  ·  analyst: Yavor  ·  Reddit r/saasforsale, asking $4,000 (over $3k budget)

Right shape (a real UK web+mobile hospitality SaaS you could operate) but the #1 growth lever is B2B restaurant/bar sales — your weakness — in a saturated market owned by POS incumbents (Toast/Square/SpotOn) and specialists (Sunday, me&u); order-management-only (no payments), no disclosed traction, over budget. growth_lever_fits=false → PASS.

## Scores (0-5)
- operability_upside: 3 (med) — Real web + native mobile SaaS on your stack; bottleneck is sales, not features.
- pmf_retention: 2 (low) — No customers/revenue disclosed; phrasing implies ~zero traction. Unverified.
- unit_economics: 2 (low) — $49.99/mo or $399.99/yr per venue (verified) but current revenue unknown/negligible.
- moat_defensibility: 1 (med) — Commodity QR ordering, NO payments; loses to POS-bundled QR and Sunday/me&u.
- growth_market: 2 (med) — Growing category but saturated and owned by POS incumbents.
- tech_ip: 3 (med) — Web + iOS + Android + backend + domain + UK Ltd; app-store transfer to confirm.
- transfer_key_person: 3 (low) — Assets impersonal and transfer; little growth engine to inherit.
- deal_terms: 2 (med) — $4k for a no-revenue commodity codebase; poor value, over budget (1.3x).
- seller_adverse_selection: 2 (med) — Built-but-couldn't-sell in a hard market; not fraud, likely no traction.

## Gates
- operable: true
- growth_lever_fits: false → PASS (kill gate: lever is B2B cold sales, this team's weakness)
- ip_transferable: unknown
- value_transfers: unknown
- not_fragile: unknown
- revenue_evidence: none → provisional

## Key unknowns
- Any paying venues / MRR? Reddit post body never read — decisive unknown.
- Why selling; what's included (app-store accounts, RoomDrop sibling, TableDrop LTD)?
- Any non-sales acquisition channel (POS partnership, directory, inbound)?
- Clean transfer of App Store / Google Play developer accounts.
- Ongoing costs (hosting, app-store fees, third-party APIs).
